overall_summary: ElizaOS is navigating a transition focused on v2.0.0 alpha stability while managing community-led infrastructure projects like zeitgaist. Technical discussions are dominated by plugin breakage in version 1.7.2 and the evaluation of three different autonomous agent implementations.
key_facts: Meme Broker released the zeitgaist project, a VPS orchestration system using Conway terminals and OpenClaw for swarm management.
Odilitime advised developers to use the v2-develop branch for production instead of the v2.0.0 alpha due to breaking changes and bcrypt issues.
Julio Holon identified that plugin-linear, plugin-rolodex, and plugin-memory are non-functional in ElizaOS version 1.7.2 without manual patching.
The ElizaOS ecosystem currently supports three autonomous implementations: plugin-autonomous, v2.0.0's built-in system, and the milaidy project.
A credit-building plugin featuring certified mail automation was introduced and recognized as a 'plugin-form candidate' by Odilitime.
Core developers clarified that the official project token is $elizaOS, following the February 4 migration deadline.
The team integrated the SAID Protocol to provide agents with verifiable digital identities on the Solana blockchain.
Major integrations for WhatsApp, Gmail, and N8N workflow engines were finalized to expand agent reach.
open_questions: Should I use ElizaOS 'v2-develop' instead of 'alpha' channel?
For autonomous cron-like behavior, do you rely on some plugin, ElizaOS 2.0 autonomy, or did you code something separate?
Does the code bot follow your org if you use it?
When does Babylon launch, and milady.ai?
How do you handle FCRA compliance verification before sending? Curious about the safeguards to prevent bad disputes.
How is it after the end of the deadline on 4.2. still possible to migrate ai16z to elizaOS token?

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Improves long-term system architecture and performance.

new_issues_prs.title: SAID Protocol for on-chain Solana identity
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Enables verifiable agent signatures and digital passports.

overall_focus.claim: The project is prioritizing stability and clearing a massive backlog of technical hurdles in preparation for a major beta launch.

discord_updates.summary: Deep dive into the zeitgaist VPS orchestration project and troubleshooting specific plugin integrations like Conway.
Debates regarding the legality of the Hyperscape (RuneScape) project and clarification on the $elizaOS token migration status.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed frustration over breaking changes in v2.0.0 alpha and the high maintenance required for plugins in version 1.7.2.
Meme Broker reported feeling overlooked by the community despite sharing advanced VPS orchestration tools.

strategic_insights.insight: The presence of three parallel autonomous implementations (V2 built-in, plugin-autonomous, and milaidy) creates confusion for developers choosing a production path.
implications_or_questions: Which autonomy standard will be the long-term focus for the core team?

strategic_insights.insight: A clear distinction is emerging between ElizaOS as a stable framework for financial/enterprise usage and OpenClaw for 'bleeding edge' browser control and autonomy.
implications_or_questions: How can ElizaOS maintain its enterprise appeal while rapid breaking changes occur in alpha?

market_analysis.observation: Identification of ai16z as an interesting token despite outdated model analysis; clarification that $elizaOS is the active token.
market_analysis.relevance: Prevents community confusion regarding token migration and market sentiment.
